_comment: Sentinel Apps catalogue — owner-only sideload distribution. Regenerated by regen_manifest.py from on-disk artifacts; per-version changelog/metadata is preserved across runs.

apps.name: Volery
apps.description: Your private AI flock. Scout runs entirely on your phone — offline, no account, no cloud. Sage connects to a bigger model you bring: your own computer over Tailscale (LM Studio / Ollama / llama.cpp) or any OpenAI-compatible cloud key. Dove is the hive (memory + tools, advanced). Your data stays on your devices. Your suite. Your AI.

versions.changelog: Build 0.58.0.

Smarter model picker. Every model now shows its download size AND estimated RAM when loaded (e.g. "3.0 GB · ≈4.3 GB RAM"). Before a model loads, if it would use more than 60% of your phone's RAM you'll get a heads-up — so you don't pick one that won't fit.
Set the support contact email (sentinellabs26@gmail.com) for the in-app "Report response" feature. Play-launch groundwork.

Report an AI response. Long-press any assistant reply and choose "Report response" to flag output that's off — it opens a prefilled email. Also fixed a long-standing annoyance: long-pressing the response text now opens the message menu (Copy/Share/Report/Delete), instead of only working on the bubble's edge.

Under-the-hood groundwork for a possible iOS version (no visible changes). Introduced a platform-agnostic networking seam (a pure-Kotlin core/ package) and routed the MCP client + web tools through it, so the biggest platform dependency — raw HTTP — is now swappable. This also makes the logic cleaner and unit-testable on Android. Verified: web tools and MCP still work exactly as before. See core/ARCHITECTURE.md for the Kotlin-Multiplatform roadmap.

MCP is real. The MCP card now connects to actual MCP servers (Streamable HTTP) — paste a server URL (+ token if needed), tap Connect, and Volery discovers its tools and caches them. Enabled servers' tools join the 🧰 chat tray and are callable by Sage via function-calling, so a whole external tool suite plugs in at once. Point it at your own metamcp or any remote server. (MCP tools run on the Sage/cloud lane, which handles their multi-field inputs; on-device Scout keeps the simple built-in tools.) Verified end-to-end against a live server.

See which tools ran. Assistant replies that used tools now show a small "🔧 used: …" footer (e.g. "used: Crypto price, Web search") — so it's clear what happened, on both the on-device Scout lane and the cloud Sage lane. Under the hood, Scout and Sage now share one tool-execution path, so a tool behaves identically whoever ran it. Verified end-to-end: Sage function-calling drove a live crypto-price lookup and answered with the result.

Tools page, decluttered. Settings ▸ Tools is now four clean cards — Native (on-device), Connector (web tools + your API keys), Self-host (your box) and MCP (coming) — instead of a long scroll of prose. The verbose explanations moved into a tooltip: tap the ⓘ on any card for the exact steps to activate those tools. Each card collapses; expand one to flip toggles. Notes and Screen translate now live tucked inside the Native card.

Timer tool fixed. "Nudge me in 5 minutes" now actually sets the timer — the app was missing the SET_ALARM permission, so the Clock request was being blocked. Added it (a normal permission, no prompt), and the timer now falls back to opening your Clock app if an OEM rejects the silent path. The tool selection itself was working all along.

Sage can now use your tools. The cloud lane drives the same Connected tools as Scout via native function-calling (OpenAI and Anthropic), so multi-step asks — search, then read a link, then answer — work end to end on a capable cloud model. The 🧰 tray still decides which tools any message may use; pick a few and Sage calls them, showing "using web search…" as it goes. On-device Scout keeps its constrained single-tool path. Under the hood, tool execution is now shared by both lanes so a tool behaves identically whoever ran it. (The box preset stays tool-free; bring an OpenAI/Anthropic key for Sage tools.)

Bring-your-own-key tools, the OpenClaw way. A new Connected tools section (Settings ▸ Tools) adds native web tools that run right on your phone: Wikipedia, read-a-link and crypto prices need no key, while web search (Tavily/Brave), weather (OpenWeatherMap) and news (NewsAPI) just need yours — and every key stays on the device. In a chat, a new 🧰 tools tray above the message box lets you pick exactly which tools each message may use; only the ones you tick get called, which keeps on-device Scout fast and accurate. Test any tool straight from Settings.

Settings, reorganised. There's now a dedicated Tools tab (Scout's tools, Notes, Translate on screen - and the future home for connecting MCP servers + more native phone tools), so Scout's tab is just the on-device brain. Cards now collapse behind a tappable header with an icon, so each tab opens short; tap to expand what you need. Related cards were merged (e.g. Engine now holds sampling + benchmark; Data & privacy combines data, security and privacy; About folds in licenses + feedback) and a duplicate storage panel was removed. Nothing was taken away - just tidier.

Screen translate, now instant - and the black-screen bug is fixed. New Fast mode (on by default) reads + translates the screen with on-device ML Kit in well under a second, no AI engine needed (Latin scripts; first use downloads a small language model). The capture pipeline was reworked so it no longer hands back a black frame on some phones. Turn Fast mode off to use Scout instead - slower (~30s) but reads any script and understands context. One-tap language picker included.

Screen-translate: clearer when a screen can't be captured. Some apps (Telegram, banking, video/DRM) block screenshots and screen capture, so they come through as a black image. The bubble now detects that and tells you why - 'this screen is protected from capture' - instead of spending 30s only to report it's blank, and it retries once in case the frame was just stale.

Screen-translate fixes. The bubble now actually translates instead of just reading the text back: a much firmer prompt forces a translation (never a transcription), and the result panel shows what it's translating into (e.g. 'Translation -> Spanish'). A new language picker lets you choose the target in one tap - and it's now clear you set the language to translate INTO, not the one on screen. Changing the language applies on the very next tap, no restart.

Translate what's on your screen. A new floating bubble (Settings > Scout > Translate on screen) sits over any app - tap it and Scout reads the current screen and translates it into the language you pick; long-press the bubble to stop. The screenshot is decoded on-device and never uploaded. You'll be asked once to allow 'display over other apps' and screen capture; the engine starts itself on the first translate. (A faster always-on auto mode is coming next.)

Privacy & safety. Turn on App lock to require your phone's PIN / pattern / fingerprint to open Volery. An Encrypted backup option now lets you save EVERYTHING - including your API keys - sealed with a password (AES-GCM) and restore it on a new phone. A Privacy card shows exactly what's granted and what's stored, with one-tap Delete everything. Plus an on-device Usage summary (chats, messages, replies per agent) and an Open-source licenses page. All on your device.

Cloud, expanded. Five new one-tap providers: Google Gemini, DeepSeek, Together AI, Mistral and xAI (Grok) - alongside OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter and Groq. A per-profile spend tracker shows roughly how many calls and tokens you've used (and the estimated cost from your prices), with a budget you can set for alerts and a reset. Save reusable system prompts to a library and reuse them in a tap, and star your favourite models so they sort to the top of the picker.

Smarter Scout setup. Before you download a model, Volery now tells you if it will run well, be tight, or won't fit on THIS phone (based on its RAM) - shown on the model and on your installed list. Big downloads keep going in the background and can be cancelled, and their progress shows again when you reopen Settings. New 'Test a tool' buttons let you check Location / Clipboard / Calendar / Notes on the spot, and a Scout's notes manager lets you read and delete what Scout remembered.

Make-it-yours, round 2. A live Preview at the top of Personalize shows your chat look as you tweak it. Avatars now support a photo (not just emoji) and cover all four agents - Scout, Sage, Dove and you. Quick looks presets (Volery / Minimal / Terminal / Classic / Big & clear) restyle the chat in one tap. The empty chat screen shows tappable starter prompts you can edit. Plus optional haptic feedback when you send.

Your data, and an About page. New App tab tools: export your settings to a file and import them on another phone (keys + tokens are left out, so it's safe to share); a storage breakdown showing what models, images, chats and cache are using; and one-tap Clear for chats, images & cache, or downloaded models (with a confirm). Plus an About card (version, build, privacy summary), a What's-new button, a Send-feedback shortcut, and a Check-for-updates-on-launch toggle.

Cloud tuning + health. Each Sage profile can now set its own temperature, max tokens, and a system-prompt override. Test connection is richer: it reports round-trip latency, the model that answered, and token usage - and if you enter your provider's $/1M prices, it estimates the cost of that call. New Check box button on the Box link shows whether your box is reachable (latency + version + tool count). Everything still on-device; keys never leave the phone.

Cloud (Sage) levelled up. Claude now works natively — a new Anthropic provider (no OpenRouter needed). Save multiple Sage profiles (e.g. OpenAI + Groq + your PC) and switch between them in a tap. Pick the model from a dropdown fetched live from your provider instead of typing it. Show/hide your API key. And add custom headers for proxies or gateways. Your keys still never leave the device.

More on-device tools for Scout, and you choose exactly which. New tools: Location (your current area, coarse), Clipboard (read what you just copied), Notes (Scout's scratchpad - 'remember this' and 'what did I note'), and Timer (set a countdown in the Clock app). Per-tool scoping: Settings > Scout > Scout's tools now lists every tool with its own on/off switch, grouped by what it needs (box link / a phone permission / nothing) - so you can allow just the ones you want. Everything stays on-device and is only ever read by on-device Scout, never the cloud lanes.

Model manager + a live catalogue. Scout > Model now lists the models already on your phone (with sizes), lets you switch the active one or delete to free space, and can hold several at once. Tap Browse models to pull the current list straight from HuggingFace (litert-community, sorted by popularity) instead of a baked-in list - pick one and it finds the best file + size for you, then downloads. Works offline too: if there's no connection it falls back to a small built-in set. The manual .litertlm URL and Import-file paths are still there.

Engine controls + live stats (no guesswork). The Scout > Engine card now shows real numbers while Scout runs - uptime, app RAM, and last-turn throughput (prefill / decode tokens-per-second + time-to-first-token), all measured by the engine, plus your live battery level. New: Stop the engine after N minutes idle, and Pause below N% battery (unless charging) - both applied live, no restart. A Sampling card lets you optionally tune temperature / top-K / top-P (off = the model's own defaults). And Test Scout runs a fixed prompt and reports this phone's actual tokens-per-second.

Compatible models. The Scout > Model card now lists a hand-picked set of on-device models that run on this engine - tap one to fill the download link, then Download: Gemma-4 E4B (the default, best quality, sees images), Gemma-4 E2B (lighter multimodal), Qwen2.5 1.5B (compact all-rounder), Gemma 3 1B (fastest, lowest RAM) and Phi-4 mini (strongest reasoning). Sizes shown; 📷 marks the ones that can also see images. Every entry is a verified litert-community model.

Make it yours, part 2b - avatars + chat backgrounds. Turn on avatars to show a round emoji beside every message: pick your own for Scout and for you (Sage shows an owl, Dove a dove). And give the conversation a backdrop - choose a built-in gradient (Sunset / Ocean / Forest / Dusk) or set one of your own photos as the chat background. Both live under Settings > Personalize.

Make it yours, part 2. Persona presets (Concise / Friendly / Coding buddy / Tutor) for Scout; a custom hex accent colour beyond the 6 presets; per-agent dot colours; and 'About you' is now structured (name / role / languages / anything else) so Scout tailors replies better. (Avatars + chat background coming next.)

More personalization (batch 1 of the settings refinements). Chat appearance now has a Font choice (System / Serif / Monospace), Bubble shape (Rounded / Square), and a Show-timestamps toggle - plus a 'Reset personalization' button to return everything to defaults. Settings -> Personalize.

Streaming + Stop. Cloud (Sage) replies now stream in token-by-token as they're written. The send button becomes a Stop button while a reply is generating - tap to stop (Scout/Dove still return the whole reply at once; the on-device model can't stream).
Message actions + richer replies. Long-press any message for Copy, Share, Delete - and Regenerate on the latest reply. Markdown now renders GFM tables, strikethrough, and auto-links bare URLs.

Lighter on launch + tidier Settings. Scout (the on-device model) no longer auto-starts - it starts the first time you message it, so there's no RAM spike on open (turn auto-start back on in Settings -> Scout -> Engine if you prefer). Settings now has a top tab bar (Personalize / Scout / Cloud / App) and a back arrow to return to chat; Save moved into the top bar.

Chat history. Tap the menu icon (top-left) to open a drawer of all your conversations - switch between them, rename, or delete, and start a new chat. Your past chats are no longer trapped. Also: Settings -> App updates now has a 'Replay welcome tour' button to see onboarding again.

Better service notification. The ongoing notification now shows a clean status line - Ready/Loading - GPU/CPU - port - and device RAM used/total, with a Stop button to pause the on-device model right from the shade (start it again from the app). Also renamed to Volery.

Clearer calendar tool. The Calendar permission now says it reads the calendars synced to this phone (which usually include Google), with a 'Don't see your Google Calendar?' helper that explains how to sync it and a shortcut to Android's sync settings.

Scout can use your phone. New on-device tools let Scout read your Calendar and Contacts to answer questions like 'what's on tomorrow?' or 'what's Sam's number?' - read on your device and used only by on-device Scout, never uploaded. Opt-in per tool in Settings -> Scout's tools (grant the permission). No server or MCP needed.

Polished Settings + Onboarding. Settings is now grouped (Personalize / On-device / Cloud & tools / App) with rounded, theme-coloured cards. Onboarding now follows your theme and accent properly (it was dark-only before, which looked wrong in light mode). Same look-and-feel as the new chat.

A prettier chat (design pass, sample). New header with the agent name, a coloured dot and a 'tap to switch' subtitle; refined message bubbles with tails that follow your theme; a rounded input pill with image + send icon buttons; an animated typing indicator; and a friendlier empty state. This is a sample of the polish direction - more screens to follow.

Make it yours. Settings now has: an accent colour picker (6 colours) on top of light/dark/Material You; Your assistant - rename Scout and give it a personality / custom instructions; About you - your name + a few details Scout uses to tailor replies (private, on-device); and Chat appearance - text size and density. Persona + About-you apply to on-device Scout only, so they never leave your phone.

Redesigned chat. The chat screen is rebuilt in Jetpack Compose and now follows your theme - light, dark or system, plus Material You (Settings -> Appearance). Cleaner bubbles, smoother input, agent-coloured replies, long-press to copy. Same chat, fresh look. (Please test: switching agents, attaching an image to Scout, and light/dark.)

New name: Volery. The app is now Volery - your private AI flock. Adds a first-run onboarding flow: a welcome, a tour of your flock (Scout on-device, Sage the model you bring, Dove the hive), a privacy promise, and a one-tap start. Settings and the app title are rebranded. Same package, so this updates in place.

Use your own computer as the smart lane. New Sage provider 'My computer (Tailscale)' lets you run a model on your PC (LM Studio / Ollama / llama.cpp) and reach it privately over Tailscale - free, and your data never leaves your devices. Comes with a step-by-step setup guide and a Get Tailscale link; uses your existing Tailscale app. Enter your machine's tailnet address + model, then Test connection.

Sage goes BYOK - bring your own model. Settings -> Sage now lets you pick an OpenAI-compatible provider (OpenAI / OpenRouter / Groq / a custom URL like LM Studio or Ollama) and paste your own API key, with a Test connection button. Your key stays on the device and is kept out of cloud backups. The Sentinel box stays a one-tap preset for the owner. This decouples the smart lane from the box - the groundwork for a public release where Scout runs on-device for free and Sage is whatever model you bring.

Three agents, by design a fallback ladder. The header toggle now cycles Scout -> Sage -> Dove. Scout = fast, on-device (offline floor). Sage = the box's big Qwen with no memory or tools (clean-slate smart lane; depends only on the model server). Dove = the hive lane (memory + tools land in a coming update). Each tier depends on strictly more than the one below it, so if the box or the hive is down you degrade gracefully instead of going dark. Each agent has its own bubble colour.

Phase D - Ask Dove. Tap the agent toggle in the chat header to switch a thread between Scout (fast, on-device) and Dove (the box's big Qwen model over the tailnet) for the harder, long-context questions. Dove's replies show in their own colour; the smart lane is text-only, so vision and tools stay Scout's job.

Device check now CONFIRMS, not just predicts. The 'This device' card adds a 'Verify it can run (load test)' button that actually loads the model and reports the real result - '✅ Confirmed: running on the GPU' (or CPU, or a failure). So instead of guessing from RAM, it proves it. (The verdict prediction still shows instantly with no model needed - any phone can install and check.) Installs over 0.10.0 in place.

New Settings (Jetpack Compose) + device detection. The config page is reorganized into clean cards - Device, Model, Engine, Connection & tools, Updates - instead of one long list. A new 'This device' card detects CPU/GPU/RAM/storage and tells you if your phone can run the on-device model (and which sizes) with a green/amber/red verdict. (App is ~8 MB larger from the Compose runtime.) The old screen is kept only for 'Import a model file'. Installs over 0.9.x in place.

Reliable tool-calling. Instead of Scout emitting fragile JSON (which kept failing as 'Let me look that up...'), it now does a quick decision step - the model just says TIME, SEARCH, or NONE - and the app runs the tool and feeds the result back. Much more consistent: date/time questions hit the time tool, and current-fact questions actually search the web. Slightly slower per message (an extra quick step) but it works. Toggle in Settings if you want it off.

Crash fix + diagnostics. The chat flow is now wrapped so an error during tool-calling becomes a message instead of crashing the app, and tool results are size-capped before going back to the model. Also: if the app does crash, it now captures the reason and shows it on the next launch - if you hit a crash, screenshot that dialog for me. Build on 0.8.2's verified self-update.

Fixes 'There was a problem while parsing the package'. The in-app update download could finish truncated/corrupt (the fast CDN dropped mid-stream over cellular), and the app installed it anyway. Now it verifies the downloaded APK (size + sha256) before installing and automatically retries over the tailnet if the file is bad. NOTE: to GET this fix, install 0.8.2 from the apps hub in your browser this once (reliable); after that, in-app self-update is verified end-to-end. Tool-calling hardening from 0.8.1 included.

Tool-calling made reliable. Fixes the raw 'TOOL_CALL: {...}' leaking into the chat - Scout sometimes wrote the search args in a slightly wrong shape, so the app now salvages it and runs the tool anyway, and a raw tool call can never show in chat again. Stronger instructions + a web-search example so Scout actually searches (instead of guessing) for current facts/dates and uses the time tool for date/time. Installs over 0.8.0 in place. If your current chat has leftover 'TOOL_CALL' text, start a New chat for a clean slate.

Bigger memory + smarter tools. Fixes the 'input token ids are too long' error: the context window default is now 4096 (was ~1k), and long chats auto-trim the oldest messages so it never overflows. The 'Max reply tokens' setting is renamed 'Max context tokens' (it sets the whole window; up to 32k - bigger = more RAM/slower). Tighter tool instructions so Scout reliably uses the time tool for date/time questions. Installs over 0.7.x in place.

Faster, more reliable self-update. Fixes the 'stuck at 0 MB' download (Android's DownloadManager was queuing the file for Wi-Fi / not traversing the VPN) by streaming the APK directly. It now pulls from the fast public CDN first (direct over the internet, not the slow tailscale relay) and falls back to the tailnet if needed. NOTE: this 0.7.0 to 0.7.1 update still comes over the tailnet (one more slow one), then future updates are quick. Scout's tools (0.7.0) unchanged.

Scout can use tools (Phase C). When you ask something that needs fresh info - the date, current events, a fact to look up - Scout now calls a box tool (time, web search) over the tailnet and answers using the result (watch for the wrench icon). Toggle in Settings: 'Let Scout use box tools'. Best-effort - if the box link is unreachable, Scout just stays local. TIP: grab this build with the new one-tap self-update (Settings > Check for updates > Download + install). More tools - your full metamcp set - come next.

In-app self-update (Phase C). Settings > App updates > Check for updates - the app asks the box link (over the tailnet) what's latest, and if there's a newer version, Download + install does it for you: it grabs the APK and hands it to the system installer (you just tap Install once - no hub visit, no file hunting). This is the FIRST build with self-update, so install it once from the hub; future updates are one tap. Installs over 0.5.0 in place.

Phase B (model & config): Settings can now DOWNLOAD the model in-app from a URL (pre-filled with the Gemma-4-E4B link) with live progress - no more manual file-push; it downloads safely beside your current model and only swaps in on success. Plus a Max reply tokens setting and an 'Allow background (battery)' button so Scout stays reachable when backgrounded. Installs over 0.4.0 in place. (Your existing model + chats are untouched.)

UI/UX polish: Scout's replies now render markdown properly (bold, lists, code) instead of showing raw **; a real app icon (recon reticle); an empty-state welcome on a fresh chat; long-press any message to copy; and the keyboard no longer covers the input box. Installs over 0.3.0 in place.

Vision in chat - the fast lane, finally usable. Tap '+ Img' to attach a screenshot or photo, add a question (or none), and Scout reads it on the GPU - translate a foreign screenshot, summarise a doc photo, describe an image. The image shows in the conversation and is saved with the thread. Pictures are downscaled before sending so it stays fast. Installs over 0.2.1 in place.

Fixes Scout 'couldn't load the model' on 0.2.0. The model was actually fine (Settings showed ready - GPU); the chat just couldn't reach its own local server because Android blocks plain-HTTP requests by default. Now permitted for the on-device endpoint, so chat connects. Update over 0.2.0 in place.

Meet Scout - the app is now a real chat agent, not just a server. Open it and chat with the on-device model (running on the GPU); your conversation is kept on the phone. Scout is the fast-lane recon member of the mesh, distinct from Dove on the box. This is slice 1: one conversation + New + Settings (the old server/model screen moved behind the Settings button). Coming next: a thread list, then the shared-brain (hive) connection. Installs over 0.1.6 in place.

Status sync fix: the in-app status line and the notification now update from the same event, so they're always in lockstep (no more 'loading model' in the app while the notification says 'ready - GPU'). GPU acceleration from 0.1.5 unchanged. Installs over 0.1.5 in place.

GPU acceleration: declares the phone's OpenCL graphics driver so the on-device model runs on the GPU instead of the slow CPU - image understanding should drop from minutes to ~30s. Plus a wake-lock so the OEM power manager can't freeze the app mid-task. Installs over v0.1.4 in place; your downloaded model and settings are kept.
Build 0.1.4.
Build 0.1.3.
Build 0.1.2.
Build 0.1.1.

apps.name: Sentinel Finance
apps.description: Net worth, bank statements, reconciliation, cash forecast and planning — the Sentinel Finance dashboard.

versions.changelog: Build 1.1.0.

apps.name: Sentinel Home
apps.description: Owner-only smart-home control + geofencing — Xiaomi, Tuya and Tapo devices via Home Assistant, with Google sign-in and location-based automation (e.g. turn on the aircon as you near home). Native Flutter; talks only to home.svc.

versions.changelog: Build 0.14.1.

Editable dashboard: Home -> Edit -> drag to reorder, resize tiles (small/wide/large), add/remove devices; persisted. Premium tiles with gradient + accent glow when on. (Native Flutter; replaces the favorites grid.)
Map picker for the home geofence: open a map (OpenStreetMap), drag to drop the pin on your real home, Set home here - works from anywhere, no need to be physically home. GPS quick-set kept.
Glanceable Home: a time-based greeting and favorites shown as a grid of tap-tiles (tap to toggle, long-press to unpin) that light up when on.
Live state: devices and sensors now update in real time (HA WebSocket bridged to the app via SSE, no polling). The HA token stays server-side; the app reconnects automatically.
Device rename (long-press a card), search (top-bar magnifier), per-room All off, and a home/away presence badge updated by the geofence.
Polish: a real Sentinel Home app icon (blue house on dark) replaces the default Flutter icon, and shimmer skeleton loaders replace the spinner while data loads.
Instant controls: toggles flip immediately (optimistic) and reconcile with HA silently in the background - no more full-screen reload after every tap. Plus haptic feedback on every control.
In-app integrations manager: Settings > Integrations shows what HA has connected + discovered devices, and Add an integration opens HAs own setup (OAuth/discovery/HACS) in an embedded WebView.

Geofence notifications: every arrival/leaving trigger posts a confirmation (e.g. Arrived home - Running: On bedroom AC), including a failure note if the scene call errors. The Test button shows it too. Requests notification permission on enable.
Geofencing UX: clearer location prompts. When GPS is off or permission is missing, a popup explains it with a one-tap shortcut to the right settings screen (was a too-subtle inline message).

Geofencing (arrival automation): pin home + radius in Settings, pick a scene to run on arrival and on leaving (e.g. AC on/off). Fires app-side on enter/exit with a Test button. No HA automation setup needed.

Aircon panel: a scene-backed On/Off card on Home (works now via your Tuya AC scenes, no integration), that auto-upgrades to a full climate panel (temp -/+, mode, fan speed, power) when a climate entity exists.
Phase C+D: favorites (tap the star to pin a device to Home), climate-forward Home section, master on/off on multi-switch devices, cover position slider, fan speed slider, light color swatches.
Device-nested rooms (Smart Life style): one card per physical device, with its sockets/switches/sensors grouped underneath instead of a flat row per entity. Tap a device to expand.

Rooms-first redesign: Home / Rooms / Settings nav, real HA rooms, per-device controls (climate dial, light brightness + warmth, cover up/stop/down, one-tap scenes, toggles), collapsible sensors, Sentinel dark theme.
Build 0.1.0.

apps.name: Sentinel Network
apps.description: Owner-only tailnet control plane — Headscale nodes, pre-auth keys, routes/exit nodes, ACL, AmneziaWG and WoL. Tailnet is the security boundary.

versions.changelog: Android: fixes native printing over the tailnet. The CUPS print server runs in WSL (its own tailnet node, 100.64.0.4) — the Sentinel Print service now targets ipp://100.64.0.4:631 instead of the host node .6, which could never reach it. ⚠️ NEW signing key: uninstall the previous Sentinel Network app first, then install this one (it will NOT update in place). Desktop unchanged from 0.3.1.

Fix: no more console-window flashes. The desktop app shells out to wsl/docker/netstat/curl on every poll; on Windows each spawn was flashing a cmd window. Now all child processes run with CREATE_NO_WINDOW — the app is fully headless. Same keystore — installs in place.

Unified navigation across both apps: left nav = service, top nav = that service's views (Overview · Tailnet · AmneziaWG · Router · Devices · Diagnostics · Local · Maintenance). Desktop (0.3.0): the 8-service layout + the AmneziaWG management section (peers, add friend/owner with inline QR, revoke). Android/dashboard: the same layout, plus read-only AmneziaWG peers + host Ports surfaced via the host bridge. Same keystore — installs in place.

Android: adds Sentinel Print — a native print service (Settings > Connected devices > Printing > Sentinel Print) that puts the home EPSON L1250 in every app's Print/Share dialog over the tailnet (no Mopria, no manual IP, works on 5G; experimental IPP to the home CUPS queue). Windows (desktop 0.2.0): new AmneziaWG section — manage the obfuscated VPN server from the app (live peer list, add friend/owner with inline QR, revoke, restart). Same keystore as 0.1.0, installs in place.

First release, now on both platforms. Android: TWA of the network dashboard, chromeless via Digital Asset Links — mint pre-auth keys, approve nodes, toggle routes/exit (tailnet is the security boundary). Windows: the Sentinel Network desktop app (Tauri) — full Headscale management via docker exec, plus Wake-on-LAN and the LAN forwarder.

apps.name: Sentinel Suite
apps.description: The owner-only launcher — one tap to every Sentinel pillar plus this Apps store.

versions.changelog: First release. Android: the owner-only Suite launcher — one tap to every Sentinel pillar plus this Apps store. Windows: the Suite desktop launcher (MSI + Setup.exe).

apps.name: Sentinel Translate Keyboard
apps.description: Private translate keyboard + on-screen translator. The keyboard translates the field you type (one tap) and an on-screen 🌐 bubble translates what's already on screen — incoming chat messages, etc. Translation runs on-device (Google ML Kit, ~59 languages, offline after pack download); target follows your phone language. Reads screen text via Accessibility (any script incl. Russian) with an OCR fallback. Optional self-hosted LibreTranslate server engine. No cloud API key.

apps.name: Sentinel Watchdog
apps.description: Service-health ops console — probes, status, restart, and incident view across every Sentinel pillar.

versions.changelog: Build 0.2.7.

Context window page: prompt-composition bar (where the budget goes before history), one-tap budget→ceiling match, ctx/KV presets, model-swap, and a graceful WSL-degrade with one-click distro warm recovery. New Brain view — per-thread context usage from the shared-memory store (corpus heft + the real last-turn fill).
Build 0.2.4.

Secrets: validity panel - flags in-sync-but-DEAD keys (a revoked key now shows DEAD/rotate, not green in-sync). Watchdog durability: alert-lifecycle leak fixed (no more zombie alerts), host/docker-engine circuit-breaker, serve-dance pinned to IPv4.
Build 0.2.2.

Reorganized Watchdog Monitor into Apps / Connectivity / System — new Tailnet, Secrets, Substrate and Tailnet-link (data-plane: netcheck UDP / peer handshake / node-online) tiles, all backed by live probes. Windows desktop (Tauri v2, signed).
First release — the Sentinel Watchdog ops console: service health, probes, restart, and incident view across every Sentinel pillar.

apps.name: SMDL IPTV
apps.description: Netflix-style IPTV browser — 11k+ free public channels (iptv-org, Free-TV, i.mjh.nz + community feeds). EPG, recording, geo-block warnings, VLC/inline playback.

versions.changelog: Build 0.2.4.
Build 0.2.4.
Fix: SMDL bottom tab bar no longer clipped by Android nav bar (WebView now pads with bottom system inset). Same stable keystore — install over v0.2.2 in place.

Stable debug keystore pinned in repo — future updates install in place. Promoted in-app inline player (hls.js/dash.js) to primary action. MIME-typed VLC intent for HLS/DASH/TS. Left drawer with alphabetical countries. Filter persistence across launches. Sticky topbar scroll fix.

apps.name: Sentinel Media TV
apps.description: The public community / Play TV build — IPTV browser over free public channels with EPG and inline playback. Wraps tv.sentinelsuite.xyz; licensing/entitlements enforced server-side. Distinct from the owner-only IPTV app.

versions.changelog: First public community / Play TV release. TWA wrapping tv.sentinelsuite.xyz/iptv; runs chromeless via Digital Asset Links. The safe, shippable community edition — entitlements/licensing enforced server-side.
